title: Monitoring of Sperm-Independent Calcium Oscillations in Immature Oocytes of Mice
author: Sae Horiike
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML

steps.title: Breed mice (2–4 mice per cage)...
steps.instruction: Breed mice (2–4 mice per cage) under the following specific pathogen-free conditions: 23 ± 1 °C, 12 h light/dark cycles (light on at 8:00 and off at 20:00), and ad libitum access to food and water. B. Preparation of glass pipettes for oocyte handling

steps.title: Cut the glass pipettes in the...
steps.instruction: Cut the glass pipettes in the middle using an ampoule glass cutter ( Figure 1 ). Figure

steps.title: Preparation of glass pipettes for oocyte handling
steps.instruction: Preparation of glass pipettes for oocyte handling.

steps.title: Extended glass pipette
steps.instruction: Extended glass pipette.

steps.title: Ampoule glass cutter
steps.instruction: Ampoule glass cutter.

steps.title: Cut glass pipette
steps.instruction: Cut glass pipette.

steps.title: Heat the cut edge of glass...
steps.instruction: Heat the cut edge of glass pipettes with the flame of an alcohol lamp to smooth.

steps.title: Hold both sides of the glass...
steps.instruction: Hold both sides of the glass pipette with one hand and a pair of curved tip tweezers.

steps.title: Heat the middle of the pipette...
steps.instruction: Heat the middle of the pipette until it is softened slightly, remove from the flame, and immediately pull both ends horizontally ( Figure 1 ).

steps.title: Cut the excess part of the...
steps.instruction: Cut the excess part of the pipette, heat, and smooth the tip slightly. The smooth tip is less likely to damage the oocytes and dishes ( Figure 1 ).

steps.title: Check the tip under a stereomicroscope...
steps.instruction: Check the tip under a stereomicroscope and choose glass pipettes with an inner diameter of approximately 150–200 μm.

steps.title: Wrap the glass pipettes with aluminum...
steps.instruction: Wrap the glass pipettes with aluminum foil, sterilize by dry-heat (180 °C, 30 min), and store in a new 15 mL tube. C. Preparation of dishes

steps.title: Place TYH medium drops (100 μL...
steps.instruction: Place TYH medium drops (100 μL per drop) in 60 mm dishes and cover these drops with liquid paraffin.

steps.title: Incubate these dishes in a CO...
steps.instruction: Incubate these dishes in a CO 2 incubator at 37 °C with 5% CO 2 for at least 60 min before collecting immature oocytes.

steps.title: Thaw M2 medium and place four...
steps.instruction: Thaw M2 medium and place four drops (100 μL per drop) in a new 60 mm dish. Note: Prepare this just before washing the oocytes. There is no need to cover M2 medium drops with liquid paraffin and to place them in the CO 2 incubator. D. Collection of immature oocytes from juvenile mice

steps.title: Place scissors and tweezers on the...
steps.instruction: Place scissors and tweezers on the bench and wear gloves (and other protective equipment as necessary) ( Figure 2A , B). Figure

steps.title: Isolation of ovaries from juvenile mice
steps.instruction: Isolation of ovaries from juvenile mice. (A) Anatomical set for abdominal exploration. (B) A stereomicroscope. (C) View after an abdominal operation (left image) and after turning over an intestine (right image) in female mice (12 days after birth). Arrowheads indicate ovaries. (D) Microscopic images of the oviduct and the uterus (left image) and isolated ovaries (right image). Scale bars, 1 mm.

steps.title: At 12, 15, and 21 days...
steps.instruction: At 12, 15, and 21 days after birth, sacrifice female mice using carbon dioxide.

steps.title: Disinfect the abdomen with 70% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Disinfect the abdomen with 70% ethanol and cut the abdominal skin of mice with a midline incision from the abdomen to the chest using large scissors.

steps.title: Pull two sides of the cut...
steps.instruction: Pull two sides of the cut skin and access the mouse peritoneum.

steps.title: Cut the peritoneum using small scissors ( Figure 2C )
steps.instruction: Cut the peritoneum using small scissors ( Figure 2C ).

steps.title: Locate the V-shaped uterus, oviducts, and...
steps.instruction: Locate the V-shaped uterus, oviducts, and ovaries by shifting the position of the gut and internal organs ( Figure 2C ).

steps.title: Hold the utero-tubal junction with a...
steps.instruction: Hold the utero-tubal junction with a pair of precision tweezers and separate the ovary from the oviduct and the uterus ( Figure 2D ).

steps.title: Clean the ovary by removing adipose...
steps.instruction: Clean the ovary by removing adipose tissues and transfer the ovary to the dish from step C2 ( Figure 2D ).

steps.title: Using a 22-G needle, dissect each...
steps.instruction: Using a 22-G needle, dissect each ovary into tissue fragments in M2 medium ( Figure 3A , B). Figure

steps.title: Collection of immature oocytes from juvenile...
steps.instruction: Collection of immature oocytes from juvenile mice and monitoring of Ca 2+ oscillations. (A) A mouth pipetting set for oocyte transfer. (B) Solution of oocytes and somatic cells after syringe treatment. Ovaries are isolated from female mice (12 days after birth). After the fragmented ovaries are treated with the syringe, oocytes and somatic cells are observed in the M2 solution (upper images). (C) Oocyte retrieval. The oocytes are collected using the mouse pipetting set. Arrowheads represent oocyte nuclei. Scale bars, 50 μm.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 1 min at 24 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 1 min at 24 °C.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Add 400 μL of L-15 medium containing 0
steps.instruction: Add 400 μL of L-15 medium containing 0.1% collagenase and incubate at 37 °C for 10 min.

steps.title: Perform tapping and then centrifuge at...
steps.instruction: Perform tapping and then centrifuge at 1,000× g for 1 min at 24 °C.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Add 200 μL of EDTA-containing PBS(-)...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of EDTA-containing PBS(-) and incubate at 37 °C for 5 min.

steps.title: Perform tapping and then centrifuge at...
steps.instruction: Perform tapping and then centrifuge at 1,000× g for 1 min at 24 °C.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant.

steps.title: After suspending for 10 min in...
steps.instruction: After suspending for 10 min in M2 medium, collect oocytes visually under a stereomicroscope using a capillary ( Figure 3C ). E. Collection of immature oocytes from adult mice

steps.title: Follow steps D1–
steps.instruction: Follow steps D1–

steps.title: After suspending for 10 min in...
steps.instruction: After suspending for 10 min in M2 medium, collect oocytes visually under a stereomicroscope using a capillary. Notes:

steps.title: Oocytes are larger than somatic cells,...
steps.instruction: Oocytes are larger than somatic cells, measuring 50 μm in diameter, and are spherical with a nucleus at their center. This distinctive shape makes them easily distinguishable from other cells. Furthermore, they are surrounded by the zona pellucida, making them resistant to physical stress. To prevent damage to the oocytes, it is important to select a glass capillary with a diameter slightly larger than the oocyte itself.

steps.title: The bond between the oocyte and...
steps.instruction: The bond between the oocyte and somatic cells is weak, allowing the oocyte to be released from ovarian fragments under syringe pressure. However, repeated syringe manipulation increases the risk of damaging the oocytes. Three manipulations with the syringe are optimal. F. Monitoring of Ca 2+ oscillations

steps.title: Incubate immature oocytes (around 100 oocytes)...
steps.instruction: Incubate immature oocytes (around 100 oocytes) in TYH medium containing the Ca 2+ -sensitive fluorescent dye Oregon green 488 BAPTA-1 AM (final concentration of 2 μM) for 15 min at 37 °C in a CO 2 incubator.

steps.title: Wash immature oocytes three times with...
steps.instruction: Wash immature oocytes three times with TYH medium (5 min each).

steps.title: Use a stage top incubator to...
steps.instruction: Use a stage top incubator to control temperature, humidity, and CO 2 concentration (5%) for live cell imaging ( Figure 4A ).

steps.title: Capture fluorescent images of the oocytes...
steps.instruction: Capture fluorescent images of the oocytes using a highly sensitive CCD camera, using the specific software to operate the camera, every 10 s ( Figure 4B ). Note: In our work, the magnification of the Olympus UIS2 UPLANFL objective lens was 10×, and a pinhole diameter (25 μm) was used for low magnification and a wider field of view. Scanning speed was capable of high-speed imaging up to 2,000 frames per second. Wavelength range was compatible with standard laser excitation lines from 405 to 640 nm. Figure

steps.title: Schematic image of time-lapse analysis
steps.instruction: Schematic image of time-lapse analysis. (A) Confocal microscope system (CSU-Frontier-SESPI). This system includes a microscope, CSU confocal scanner unit, and incubator. (B) Time-lapse acquisition. The CSU confocal scanner unit produces data suitable for the quantitative analysis of large datasets. Images captured in Z-stacks can be easily reconstructed into 3D models for analysis. A captured image is shown on the right.

steps.title: Measure fluorescence intensity by using the imaging software Andor iQ
steps.instruction: Measure fluorescence intensity by using the imaging software Andor iQ. Subtract each fluorescent image (F) from the image before injection or the image with the lowest fluorescence intensity (F0). Keep all acquisition parameters constant within each experiment to allow quantitative comparison between oocytes ( Figure 5 ). Figure

steps.title: Time-lapse processing and data analysis
steps.instruction: Time-lapse processing and data analysis. The data is processed using analysis software (Andor iQ). The red circles indicate oocytes. A single analysis can quantify the fluorescence intensity of multiple oocytes. The image exhibits three modes (movie editor, calibration, and analysis).

steps.title: Measure the fluorescence intensity for individual...
steps.instruction: Measure the fluorescence intensity for individual oocytes within a user-selected region that covers the majority of the area of each oocyte.

steps.title: The mean intensity over the same...
steps.instruction: The mean intensity over the same area for each of the images in a time series is analyzed automatically.

steps.title: Report changes in fluorescence intensity as the F/F0 ratio
steps.instruction: Report changes in fluorescence intensity as the F/F0 ratio. Note: Exposure time was optimized to maximize signal intensity while avoiding pixel saturation. Typical exposure times ranged from 50 to 300 ms, depending on probe brightness and labeling efficiency. Saturation levels were monitored using the Andor iQ intensity histogram, and acquisition settings were adjusted to ensure that the dynamic range of the camera was fully utilized without clipping. Excitation light intensity was minimized to reduce photobleaching and phototoxicity, particularly important for large and optically sensitive mouse oocytes. Z-stack parameters were defined to encompass the entire oocyte volume. Optical sections were acquired at intervals of 1 μm, depending on the objective numerical aperture and axial resolution. Maximum intensity projections or single optical sections were generated post-acquisition using Andor iQ analysis tools.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol has been used and validated in the following research article: • Horiike et al. [9]. Biological significance of sperm-independent calcium oscillations in immature oocytes of mice. MicroPubl Biol 2025, 2025. https://doi.org/10.17912/micropub.biology.001748
